findings.description: Spec R4.S1 says 'log entry whose message contains the literal `convergence.synthesis_failed_with_checkpoint`'. This is fragile to log-message-formatter changes (e.g., a logging.Formatter that mangles the message). A more durable test contract is to assert on the structured payload's `event` key (e.g., extra={'event': 'convergence.synthesis_failed_with_checkpoint', ...}) rather than scanning message text.
findings.resolution: Update the spec to require an `event` key in the structured `extra={}` payload set to a stable string identifier, and assert on that key in tests rather than message-text scanning. The literal string can still appear in the message for human readability, but the test contract is the structured key.

findings.description: Task 5.1 references 'skill-workflow.R4.S1 (audit emitted)' but R4.S1 was renamed to 'synthesis failure emits log entry' in iteration 3 (no longer audit). The reference is correct (R4.S1 still exists with new wording), but the task's parenthetical description is stale.
findings.resolution: Update task 5.1 description: skill-workflow.R4.S1 (synthesis failure log entry emitted), not 'audit emitted'.

findings.description: Path safety regex `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+` for vendor names was verified compliant with current vendor names (claude_code, gpt-5-codex, gemini). But it would reject names with periods (e.g., 'claude.opus-4.7'), version qualifiers ('claude-3.7-sonnet@v2'), or namespaces ('anthropic/claude-3'). Such names are common in vendor configs and may appear in future. The regex is defensive but possibly TOO defensive.
findings.resolution: Document the trade-off in design.md D6: the regex prioritizes filename safety over name expressiveness; if future vendor names need periods or @-tags, expand the regex (period and @ are filesystem-safe, but `:`, `/`, `\`, NUL, leading `..` are not).

findings.description: R1.S5 (atomic manifest write) covers the manifest with parent-dir fsync, but R1.S1 (round-trip preserves vendor findings) doesn't explicitly require per-vendor finding files to be written atomically too. Tasks.md task 0.2 says both use atomic-rename, but the spec only mandates it for the manifest.
findings.resolution: Update R1 (the requirement statement before scenarios) to require atomic-rename + parent-dir fsync for BOTH per-vendor finding files AND the manifest. Or add a new scenario R1.S{N}: 'Per-vendor finding-file write is atomic'.
